allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives step-back the whole-machine view that turns a pause into a better structural diagnosis rather than into generic reflection.
allies.source_quote: Stepping back reveals the whole system, the interrelationships between parts, and the patterns of change.

allies.rationale_text: Metacognitive-questioning is the internal mechanism of step-back, because the pause works by inspecting how thinking is proceeding and what it is missing.
allies.source_quote: The act of stepping back is metacognition.

allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking helps step-back strip away assumptions and recover the deeper structure before the next decision is made.
allies.source_quote: The step back is used to strip away assumptions and conventions, returning the problem to its fundamental facts and desired outcomes.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias turns the pause into a defense of the current hypothesis instead of a real reconsideration of whether the frame or direction is wrong.
antagonists.source_quote: rigidly defending an early hypothesis blocks the reconsideration that the pause is designed to enable

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias replaces reflective reset with default continuation, making the pause ceremonial rather than decision-improving.
antagonists.source_quote: step-back conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when reflective pause is replaced by default continuation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Step-back conflicts with commitment-bias when a prior public stance blocks the reconsideration that the pause is supposed to create.
structured_tensions.source_quote: step-back vs commitment-and-consistency-bias: step-back conflicts with commitment-and-consistency-bias (commitment and consistency bias) when prior public stance blocks reconsideration.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Step-back conflicts with status-quo-bias when reflective pause is replaced by default continuation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: step-back vs status-quo-bias: step-back conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when reflective pause is replaced by default continuation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Step-back conflicts with confirmation-bias when rigidly defending an early hypothesis blocks the reconsideration that the pause is designed to enable.
structured_tensions.source_quote: step-back vs confirmation-bias: step-back conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when rigidly defending an early hypothesis blocks the reconsideration that the pause is designed to enable.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on pause-for-structure, metacognitive reset, and an explicit tension block around anti-reconsideration biases, so the file preserves that compact surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor reflection relations beyond the strongest current-vocabulary links around systems, self-audit, and anti-default-continuation pressure.
open_questions: Outside-view pressure is source-real, but the retained set favors already-curated models with cleaner direct wording in the source.
